meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve can't deploy apps (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome can't deploy apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Skills Setup to Solve Deployment
content.seo_description: Master Claude Code skills setup to overcome deployment hurdles. A beginner-friendly checklist for students using Antigravity to launch your first SaaS app.
content.answer_block: Deploying apps fails when local environments drift from production settings. By mastering Claude Code, you automate the infrastructure-as-code process, ensuring your local codebase maps perfectly to cloud providers. This setup synchronizes your development workflow, eliminates configuration errors, and guarantees your project launches on the first attempt, every single time.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect logic, only to watch your terminal vomit a wall of red error text the moment you attempt to deploy. The bridge between a functional local build and a live, public-facing application is where most student projects go to die. It’s not a lack of coding talent; it’s a failure of the deployment pipeline.

Today, we stop the cycle of 'it works on my machine.' By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you gain an autonomous engineering partner that handles the heavy lifting of configuration and environment syncing. We are moving beyond manual copy-pasting and into the era of AI-orchestrated infrastructure.

steps.title: Initialize Environment Intelligence
steps.description: Establish a base config that allows Claude Code to 'see' your project structure and identify potential deployment blockers early.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current repository structure and identify any missing configuration files required for a standard Vercel or Railway deployment.

steps.title: Automate Dependency Mapping
steps.description: Sync your local dependencies with production-grade requirements to prevent runtime crashes during build phases.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my package.json and environment variables to ensure all production-level dependencies are explicitly defined and locked.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'audit dependencies and create a build-ready lockfile'

steps.title: Configure Infrastructure-as-Code
steps.description: Generate the necessary IaC files so your deployment target understands exactly how to host your application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready Dockerfile and a vercel.json configuration file tailored for this specific project architecture.

steps.title: Simulate Production Environment
steps.description: Run a dry-run build locally to catch environment variable mismatches before pushing to the cloud.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Simulate a production build process and flag any environment variables that are missing or improperly formatted.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'simulate production build and report errors'

steps.title: Finalize Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Execute the final push and verify that the build succeeds by monitoring the logs through the CLI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the deployment logs and provide a summary of the successful build steps and any final optimization suggestions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'verify build success and optimize startup time'

pro_tips: Always use a .env.example file; never let Claude push real secrets to your public repository.
Use the 'claude --verbose' flag when debugging deployment to see exactly which shell command is failing.
Ask Claude to generate a 'deployment-readiness' checklist specific to your chosen cloud provider.
Keep your build context small; use a .dockerignore or .gitignore to prevent massive node_modules from slowing down build steps.
Treat your deployment config as code—version control your infrastructure files just as strictly as your application logic.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, focus on setting up a CI/CD pipeline so that every Git commit automatically triggers a new deployment build.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to stop struggling with deployment and start scaling your ideas. Don't let your code sit hidden on your laptop. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
